name: 3CX Desktop App
notes: The 2023 3CX supply chain attack (Lazarus Group/AppleJeus) was the first documented cascading supply chain compromise; trojanized Windows and macOS installers distributed to 600,000+ customers.

name: Active Directory
notes: Primary target for lateral movement, privilege escalation, and persistence; exploited via Kerberoasting, Pass-the-Hash, and DCSync attacks across virtually all ransomware campaigns.

name: Adobe Acrobat / Reader
notes: Frequently exploited via malicious PDF payloads for initial access; multiple CVEs added to CISA KEV; common phishing lure delivery mechanism.

name: Android
notes: 6 Android CVEs added to CISA KEV in 2024; targeted by spyware vendors (Pegasus, Predator), APT-C-23 mobile RATs, and commercial surveillance tools; Android Pixel Privilege escalation heavily exploited.

name: AnyDesk
notes: Widely abused by ransomware operators and BEC actors as a legitimate remote access tool to maintain persistent access and evade detection; seen in DARKGATE and LockBit campaigns.

name: Apache HTTP Server
notes: Persistently targeted via path traversal, mod_cgi, and RCE vulnerabilities; high KEV representation from Apache Foundation; broadly deployed as internet-facing web server.

name: Apache Struts
notes: CVE-2017-5638 (Equifax breach) and CVE-2023-50164 remain hallmarks of Java framework exploitation; frequently leveraged for unauthenticated RCE via OGNL injection.

name: Apache Tomcat
notes: Widely exploited via deserialization, AJP (Ghostcat CVE-2020-1938), and JSP upload vulnerabilities; common in enterprise Java environments targeted by APTs.

name: Apple iOS / iPadOS
notes: 5 Apple iOS CVEs in 2024 CISA KEV; repeatedly targeted via zero-click exploits (FORCEDENTRY, CVE-2023-41064) by Pegasus spyware and state-sponsored actors against journalists and dissidents.

name: Apple macOS
notes: Targeted by DPRK macOS malware (RustBucket, KANDYKORN), Lazarus Group cryptocurrency theft campaigns, and adware/stealers; XProtect bypasses and GateKeeper evasion are recurring themes.

name: Atlassian Confluence
notes: CVE-2021-26084, CVE-2022-26134, and CVE-2023-22527 (CVSS 10.0) have been mass-exploited for cryptomining, ransomware, and initial access; heavily represented in CISA KEV.

name: AWS (Amazon Web Services)
notes: AWS credential theft (IAM key exposure, S3 misconfigurations, EC2 IMDS abuse) is a primary cloud attack vector; Pacu and CloudFox frameworks observed in Mandiant IR engagements targeting AWS.

name: Azure Active Directory / Entra ID
notes: Targeted for golden SAML attacks, token theft, and OAuth abuse; UNC3944 and APT29 both exploited federated identity to persist across M365 and Azure environments.

name: Barracuda Email Security Gateway
notes: CVE-2023-2868 was exploited as a zero-day from at least October 2022; Mandiant's 3rd most prevalent 2023 vulnerability; Barracuda advised full appliance replacement due to deep persistence.

name: BeyondTrust PRA / Remote Support
notes: CVE-2024-12356 exploited in December 2024 compromising the U.S. Treasury Department via BeyondTrust's Remote Support SaaS; critical authentication bypass enabling RCE.

name: BloodHound
notes: Open-source Active Directory reconnaissance tool routinely abused by ransomware operators and APT groups to map attack paths for privilege escalation.

name: Brute Ratel C4
notes: Commercial adversary simulation framework adopted by Black Basta and Qakbot-linked threat actors; designed specifically to evade EDR telemetry through direct syscalls.

name: Check Point Quantum / Gaia
notes: CVE-2024-24919 (information disclosure) was exploited in 2024 campaigns to deploy ShadowPad ransomware; Check Point perimeter devices are recurring targets for initial access.

name: Cisco ASA / FTD
notes: Cisco ASA/FTD has 5 entries in top-10 2024 CISA KEV products; CVE-2018-0171 and CVE-2023-20198 exploited by Salt Typhoon and other threat actors for persistent access.

name: Cisco IOS / IOS XE
notes: CVE-2023-20198 and CVE-2023-20273 (IOS XE web UI RCE) were zero-day exploited by Salt Typhoon to implant backdoors on thousands of internet-facing Cisco routers.

name: Citrix NetScaler ADC / Gateway
notes: CVE-2023-4966 (CitrixBleed) and CVE-2023-3519 both in CISA KEV; CVE-2023-4966 exploited by LockBit and Medusa for session token hijacking across healthcare and finance sectors.

name: Citrix ShareFile / StorageZones
notes: CVE-2023-24489 (ShareFile StorageZones unauthenticated RCE) exploited in 2023; ShareFile is a recurring target for ransomware actors seeking access to enterprise file-sharing infrastructure.

name: Cleo Harmony / VLTrader / LexiCom
notes: CVE-2024-50623 exploited by the Cl0p ransomware group in December 2024, leading to extortion of at least 66 victim organizations; a follow-on to the MOVEit/GoAnywhere MFT exploit wave.

name: Cobalt Strike
notes: Most frequently observed malware/tool in Mandiant investigations (10% of all intrusions in 2023); the de facto post-exploitation C2 framework for both nation-state actors and ransomware groups globally.

name: ConnectWise ScreenConnect
notes: CVE-2024-1708 and CVE-2024-1709 (authentication bypass + path traversal) were chained by ransomware groups in the first publicly reported exploitation of ScreenConnect vulnerabilities in 2024.

name: CrowdStrike Falcon
notes: The July 2024 faulty sensor update (CSAgent) caused a global outage affecting 8.5 million Windows systems; Falcon is also deliberately targeted by attackers seeking to identify and disable EDR.

name: CyberArk PAM
notes: CyberArk vaults are targeted by advanced threat actors during lateral movement to access privileged credentials; compromised CyberArk credentials provide keys to an entire enterprise.

name: D-Link Routers
notes: D-Link had 14 exploited CVEs in 2024 threat intelligence datasets; numerous end-of-life D-Link devices are botnet targets due to persistent authentication bypass and command injection flaws.

name: Docker
notes: Targeted by TeamTNT and similar cloud-focused threat actors for cryptomining and lateral movement; misconfigured Docker API endpoints and exposed sockets are a common initial access vector.

name: Elastic (Elasticsearch)
notes: Unauthenticated Elasticsearch instances have been mass-targeted for data exfiltration; recurring target in cloud misconfiguration campaigns and ransom-wipe attacks.

name: F5 BIG-IP
notes: CVE-2020-5902, CVE-2021-22986, and CVE-2022-1388 (iControl REST RCE) widely exploited by nation-state and ransomware actors; heavily represented in CISA KEV.

name: Fortinet FortiClient EMS
notes: CVE-2023-48788 (SQL injection → RCE) was the 4th most exploited CVE in Mandiant's 2024 IR engagements; FIN8 and other financially motivated actors deployed ransomware via this flaw.

name: Fortinet FortiGate
notes: FortiOS SSL-VPN CVEs (CVE-2022-42475, CVE-2023-27997, CVE-2024-21762) are among the most actively exploited edge device flaws; fourth-most exploited CVE family in Mandiant 2024 IR engagements.

name: Fortinet FortiManager
notes: CVE-2024-47575 (FortiJump) was a zero-day exploited by a threat actor compromising dozens of organizations before public disclosure; UNC3886 planted REPTILE/TABLEFLIP rootkits on FortiManager.

name: GitLab
notes: CVE-2023-7028 (account takeover via email) and CVE-2021-22205 (RCE) placed GitLab in CISA KEV; source code repositories are high-value targets for credential and secrets theft.

name: GoAnywhere MFT
notes: CVE-2023-0669 was exploited by Cl0p ransomware group as a zero-day in early 2023, resulting in over 130 known victim organizations; a major driver of the 2023 DBIR's vulnerability exploitation surge.

name: Google Chrome / Chromium
notes: 9 entries in 2024 CISA KEV (Google Chromium); frequent zero-day exploitation by nation-state actors (APT groups) targeting browser engine components for initial access via drive-by.

name: Internet Explorer / MSHTML
notes: MSHTML (CVE-2021-40444, CVE-2023-36584) remains exploited in Office documents and HTML attachments despite IE retirement; CVE-2023-32046 added to CISA KEV in 2023.

name: Internet Information Services (IIS)
notes: IIS is frequently targeted via ASP.NET deserialization, web shell deployment, and ProxyLogon/ProxyShell exploitation on Exchange/IIS; ProxyNotShell and OWASSRF also impacted IIS-hosted apps.

name: Ivanti Connect Secure
notes: CVE-2023-46805 and CVE-2024-21887 were the 2nd most exploited vulnerability pair in Mandiant's 2024 IR; exploited by 8 distinct threat clusters including five China-nexus espionage groups.

name: Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile
notes: CVE-2023-35078 (unauthenticated API access) exploited by APT groups to access Norwegian government networks; EPMM/MobileIron MDM platforms are high-value targets for credential theft.

name: JBoss / WildFly
notes: Legacy JBoss deserialization flaws (CVE-2017-12149) remain exploited years after disclosure; WildFly/JBoss deployments in financial and healthcare sectors are frequent APT targets.

name: Jenkins
notes: CVE-2024-23897 (arbitrary file read to RCE) and multiple older deserialization CVEs exploited for initial access and supply chain attacks; exposed Jenkins instances are prime targets.

name: JetBrains TeamCity
notes: CVE-2023-42793 exploited by APT29 and North Korean groups to compromise CI/CD pipelines and inject malicious code into build artifacts; Mandiant cited it in the 2024 M-Trends campaign list.

name: Juniper Junos OS
notes: CVE-2023-36844 and related J-Web chain vulnerabilities exploited actively; Juniper SRX firewalls targeted by threat actors for VPN initial access and network pivoting.

name: Kaseya VSA
notes: CVE-2021-30116 exploited by REvil/Sodinokibi in the July 2021 supply chain ransomware attack; approximately 1,500 downstream MSP customers compromised through a single SQL injection.

name: Kerberos
notes: CrowdStrike observed a 583% increase in Kerberoasting attacks in 2023; Kerberos ticket abuse (Golden Ticket, Silver Ticket, Pass-the-Ticket) is a core technique in nearly all Windows-environment APT campaigns.

name: Kubernetes
notes: Misconfigured Kubernetes API servers and exposed dashboards are exploited by cryptomining groups (TeamTNT) and APTs; privilege escalation to cluster-admin is a recurring incident response finding.

name: Linux Kernel
notes: 4 Linux Kernel CVEs added to CISA KEV in 2024; privilege escalation (DirtyCow, Dirty Pipe, netfilter flaws) routinely exploited post-initial access on server and cloud workloads.

name: LNK / Shortcut Files
notes: Weaponized LNK files are the dominant phishing payload after Microsoft blocked Office macros in 2022; used by APT28, Lazarus Group, and numerous ransomware operators for initial execution.

name: Log4j / Log4Shell
notes: CVE-2021-44228 (Log4Shell) triggered the broadest mass-exploitation event of 2021-2022; remains actively exploited by Lazarus Group and ransomware actors years after disclosure.

name: Metasploit Framework
notes: One of the most frequently observed attack frameworks globally; seen in Mandiant IR data alongside Cobalt Strike; used across all threat actor categories from script kiddies to nation-state actors.

name: Microsoft .NET Framework
notes: Deserialization vulnerabilities in .NET and ASP.NET (CVE-2017-9248, ViewState exploitation) are routinely exploited for RCE on IIS-hosted applications; frequently seen in web-shell delivery chains.

name: Microsoft Defender / Windows Defender
notes: Actively targeted for evasion and disabling by ransomware (Black Basta, LockBit) and APT groups; CVE-2023-36033 (Defender DWM privilege escalation) exploited as a zero-day.

name: Microsoft Exchange Server
notes: ProxyLogon (CVE-2021-26855) and ProxyShell remain among the most mass-exploited vulnerabilities in history; Exchange is the 2nd most-exploited product by CVE count in CISA KEV.

name: Microsoft Office / Microsoft 365
notes: Office documents (CVE-2023-36884, macro-embedded docs, OneNote, LNK) are the dominant phishing payload; Microsoft 365 credential theft and BEC is the top-ranked breach pattern in Verizon DBIR.

name: Microsoft OneNote
notes: Emerged as a primary phishing payload container in 2023 after macro blocking; QakBot, AsyncRAT, and multiple RATs delivered via malicious .one file attachments (T1566.001).

name: Microsoft SharePoint
notes: CVE-2023-24955 and multiple Server-Side Request Forgery chains in SharePoint Server exploited for RCE; attackers frequently use SharePoint for data exfiltration and as a phishing payload host.

name: Microsoft Teams
notes: APT29 (Midnight Blizzard) and DARKGATE operators conducted spear-phishing via compromised Microsoft Teams accounts in 2023; Storm-1811 used Teams for vishing-based initial access.

name: Microsoft Windows
notes: Most-targeted OS in CISA KEV by a wide margin (22 additions in 2024 alone); exploited for privilege escalation, lateral movement, and persistence via CLFS, Win32k, and NTLM hash flaws.

name: MikroTik RouterOS
notes: Routinely recruited into Meris and other large-scale DDoS botnets; used by Volt Typhoon and other APTs as SOHO router proxies for victim-network covert relay infrastructure.

name: Mimikatz
notes: The most widely used credential-harvesting tool across threat actor categories; used in virtually every Windows-based intrusion for NTLM hash extraction and Kerberos ticket manipulation.

name: MongoDB
notes: Exposed, unauthenticated MongoDB instances are systematically compromised for data exfiltration and ransom-wipe campaigns; large numbers of internet-facing instances remain unprotected.

name: MOVEit Transfer
notes: CVE-2023-34362 was Mandiant's most prevalent 2023 vulnerability; Cl0p ransomware group exploited it as a zero-day in May 2023, affecting 1,000+ organizations globally and driving the 180% DBIR spike.

name: MySQL / MariaDB
notes: Exposed MySQL instances targeted by GandCrab and other ransomware families via brute force; SQL injection leading to MySQL file read/write is a persistent web application attack vector.

name: Netgear Routers
notes: Multiple CISA KEV entries for Netgear routers; targeted by Volt Typhoon for SOHO proxy infrastructure and commonly recruited into botnets via default credential abuse.

name: Nginx
notes: Misconfigurations and vulnerabilities in Nginx (CVE-2017-7529, HTTP/2 rapid reset) are exploited for DoS and data leakage; frequently seen as the entry point in web application breaches.

name: Node.js / npm
notes: npm package supply chain attacks (typosquatting, dependency confusion, malicious maintainer takeovers) are a high-frequency threat intelligence topic; node.js prototype pollution CVEs also exploited.

name: Okta
notes: Compromised by LAPSUS$ in 2022 and again in 2023 via support system access; Scattered Spider (UNC3944) routinely phishes Okta help desks to bypass MFA and pivot to downstream tenants.

name: Oracle Database
notes: 14 Oracle CVEs in 2024 exploited products list; Oracle E-Business Suite CVE-2022-21587 was Mandiant's 2nd most prevalent 2023 vulnerability; Oracle DB default credential attacks are persistent.

name: Oracle WebLogic
notes: CVE-2020-14882 (unauthenticated RCE) and multiple T3/IIOP deserialization CVEs repeatedly exploited by crypto-mining groups and ransomware actors; WebLogic has over 14 CISA KEV entries.

name: Palo Alto PAN-OS
notes: CVE-2024-3400 (GlobalProtect command injection) was the #1 most exploited vulnerability across all 2024 Mandiant IR engagements; 6 PAN-OS CVEs added to CISA KEV in 2024.

name: PHP
notes: CVE-2024-4577 (PHP-CGI Windows RCE) was mass-exploited shortly after disclosure; PHP file inclusion, deserialization, and object injection vulnerabilities are endemic across web applications.

name: PostgreSQL
notes: CVE-2024-1597 (SQL injection bypass) and misconfigurations in pg_hba.conf lead to mass compromise; PostgreSQL COPY TO/FROM PROGRAM is abused for OS command execution in lateral movement.

name: PowerShell
notes: Top MITRE ATT&CK sub-technique (T1059.001); used in virtually every Windows-based attack for execution, lateral movement, and AMSI bypass; a core component of most post-exploitation toolchains.

name: PsExec
notes: Canonical living-off-the-land lateral movement tool; used by Conti, LockBit, and dozens of other ransomware groups to propagate payloads across Windows environments.

name: Python / PyPI
notes: PyPI supply chain attacks via typosquatting and malicious packages are a documented vector for credential theft and backdoor deployment; Python runtime also used in attack toolchains.

name: QNAP QTS / NAS
notes: 5 QNAP QTS entries in top-10 2024 exploited products; Deadbolt, Qlocker, and eCh0raix ransomware families specifically target internet-exposed QNAP devices via HBS and SMB CVEs.

name: Redis
notes: Unauthenticated Redis instances exploited for remote code execution via CONFIG rewrite and cron job techniques; widely abused by cryptomining threat actors and used as C2 relay.

name: Rockwell Automation / Allen-Bradley
notes: CISA has issued multiple advisories for Rockwell ControlLogix and FactoryTalk CVEs; critical infrastructure OT targeting by Volt Typhoon and Sandworm includes Allen-Bradley PLC systems.

name: Schneider Electric Modicon
notes: Modbus protocol attacks and HMI vulnerabilities targeting Schneider Modicon PLCs have been exploited by Incontroller/Pipedream ICS malware; recurring subject of CISA ICS-CERT advisories.

name: Siemens SIMATIC / TIA Portal
notes: Siemens SIMATIC S7 PLCs were the original target of Stuxnet; ongoing CVEs in TIA Portal and S7 protocol implementation targeted by nation-state actors in critical infrastructure campaigns.

name: Sliver C2
notes: Open-source C2 framework increasingly adopted by APT groups and ransomware operators as a Cobalt Strike alternative; observed in nation-state and ransomware campaigns by Microsoft and CrowdStrike.

name: SolarWinds Orion
notes: The SUNBURST supply chain attack (2020) compromised 18,000+ organizations via trojanized Orion updates; attributed to APT29/SVR and considered the most damaging supply chain attack on record.

name: SonicWall Firewalls / SMA
notes: SonicWall SSL-VPN accounts were the initial access vector for Fog and Akira ransomware attacks in 2024; CVE-2025-23006 added to CISA KEV in 2025; historically targeted by multiple ransomware groups.

name: Sophos XG / Firewall
notes: CVE-2020-12271 (SQL injection for zero-day RCE) exploited by suspected Chinese APT to plant Asnarök backdoor; Sophos devices repeatedly targeted for VPN initial access by multiple threat actors.

name: Spring Framework / Spring Boot
notes: CVE-2022-22965 (Spring4Shell) and CVE-2022-22963 (Spring Cloud Function RCE) exploited across Java web application deployments; heavily present in enterprise microservices environments.

name: SQL Server (MSSQL)
notes: Abused via SQL injection chains leading to xp_cmdshell RCE; commonly targeted in network intrusions for credential harvesting and lateral movement within enterprise environments.

name: Synology DiskStation Manager
notes: eCh0raix ransomware specifically targets Synology NAS devices; critical RCE vulnerabilities demonstrated at Pwn2Own 2024 (CVE-2024-10443) highlight persistent exposure of backup infrastructure.

name: TeamViewer
notes: Widely abused for unauthorized remote access persistence by ransomware operators and BEC actors; stolen TeamViewer credentials sold on darknet markets and used for initial access.

name: Telegram
notes: Preferred C2 and exfiltration channel for infostealers, RATs, and credential-harvesting tools; Mandiant observed attackers forwarding stolen credentials and TOTP codes via Telegram channels.

name: TP-Link Routers
notes: Volt Typhoon specifically used TP-Link SOHO routers as hop-point infrastructure; multiple CISA KEV entries for TP-Link command injection vulnerabilities enabling botnet recruitment.

name: Veeam Backup & Replication
notes: CVE-2024-40711 (CVSS 9.8) exploited to deploy Akira, Fog, and Frag ransomware; CVE-2023-27532 previously exploited by Cuba, EstateRansomware, and FIN7; over 20% of Rapid7 2024 IR cases involved Veeam.

name: VMware ESXi
notes: Dedicated ESXi ransomware encryptors (Black Basta, LockBit, Akira) target hypervisor mass-encryption; CVE-2024-37085 exploited by Storm-0506, Manatee Tempest, and Octo Tempest in 2024.

name: VMware vCenter Server
notes: 4 VMware vCenter CVEs added to CISA KEV in 2024; CVE-2021-21985 and CVE-2023-34048 exploited by ransomware groups and APTs for VMware infrastructure compromise and lateral movement.

name: VMware Workspace ONE / Horizon
notes: CVE-2021-22054 and Log4Shell exploitation of VMware Horizon were mass-exploited by Iranian APT groups (TunnelVision) and Chinese threat actors for persistent access.

name: WinRAR
notes: CVE-2023-38831 (malicious archive RCE) was a zero-day exploited by at least four APT groups including DarkPink and APT29 to execute code when opening booby-trapped ZIP/RAR archives.

name: WMI (Windows Management Instrumentation)
notes: Top MITRE ATT&CK living-off-the-land technique (T1047); used for lateral movement, persistence (WMI subscriptions), and process execution by virtually all advanced ransomware and APT operators.

name: WordPress
notes: Most widely attacked CMS globally; plugin supply-chain attacks (30+ plugins backdoored in 2025), brute force on wp-admin, and xmlrpc.php abuse make it a persistent top web-threat target.

name: Zimbra Collaboration Suite
notes: CVE-2022-37042, CVE-2023-37580 (reflected XSS for credential theft) in CISA KEV; Zimbra webmail is heavily targeted by nation-state actors (TA473, Winter Vivern) for email access and credential theft.
